Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Farzin Ratan Karma
Counsel: Hoo Sheau Peng Judge of the High Court Yap Neng Boo Jimmy

Facts.Text: Farzin Ratan Karma was appointed a director of MC Corporate Services Pte Ltd
Farzin Ratan Karma was appointed a director of MC Accounting Services Pte Ltd
The Directors’ Agreement provided for Helen Campos to pay Farzin Ratan Karma a lump sum of S$350,000 in settlement of possible claims or liabilities
Farzin Ratan Karma and Helen Campos entered into the Directors’ Agreement

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the actions of the majority shareholder in conducting rights issues and drawing salaries were commercially unfair to the minority shareholder
Party_Details.Rule: A shareholder's legitimate expectations are determined by the company's constitution and any agreements between shareholders
Party_Details.Application: The rights issues were a mala fide exercise to dilute his shares and that the Defendant's salaries were improperly drawn. the rights issues were necessary due to cash flow issues and that the salaries were justified
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the rights issues were justified due to the companies' financial needs and that the drawing of salaries was consistent with the Directors’ Agreement.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Helen Campos

Facts.Text: Helen Campos incorporated MC Corporate Services Pte Ltd
Helen Campos was the sole shareholder and director of MC Corporate Services Pte Ltd at incorporation
MC Accounting Services Pte Ltd was incorporated on 2008-01-29, and Helen Campos was its sole shareholder at incorporation
Helen Campos agreed to pay S$350,000 to Farzin Ratan Karma under the Directors’ Agreement in settlement of possible claims or liabilities
Helen Campos and Farzin Ratan Karma entered into the Directors’ Agreement

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the majority shareholder's actions in conducting rights issues and drawing salaries were commercially unfair to the minority shareholder
Party_Details.Rule: A shareholder's legitimate expectations are determined by the company's constitution and any agreements between shareholders
Party_Details.Application: The rights issues were necessary due to cash flow issues and that the salaries were justified. the rights issues were a mala fide exercise to dilute his shares and that the Defendant's salaries were improperly drawn
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the rights issues were justified due to the companies' financial needs and that the drawing of salaries was consistent with the Directors’ Agreement.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Helen Campos

Facts.Text: MCCS entered into an agreement with FEL concerning the salary of Asis Rose Dela Rosa
FEL did not pay MCCS the agreed monthly sum of S$1,700 for Rose's services from January 2021 to July 2021

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the director contravened his duty by causing the company to enter into an agreement that was not in its best interest
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in good faith and in the best interests of the company, avoiding conflicts of interest
Party_Details.Application: The director caused the company to enter into an agreement with his personal company, which then failed to make payments. the agreement was terminated as Rose ceased working for his company
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the agreement was not properly terminated and that the director's personal company was liable for the unpaid amounts.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Farzin Ratan Karma
Counsel: Hoo Sheau Peng Judge of the High Court Yap Neng Boo Jimmy

Facts.Text: On 2020-01-10, Mr Karma caused MCCS to enter into Rose's Salary Agreement with FEL concerning Asis Rose Dela Rosa's salary
From January 2021 until July 2021, Mr Karma's personal company, FEL, did not pay MCCS the agreed monthly sum of S$1,700 for Rose's services

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the director contravened his duty by causing the company to enter into an agreement that was not in its best interest
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in good faith and in the best interests of the company, avoiding conflicts of interest
Party_Details.Application: The agreement was terminated as Rose ceased working for his company. the director caused the company to enter into an agreement with his personal company, which then failed to make payments
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the agreement was not properly terminated and that the director's personal company was liable for the unpaid amounts.
